verses.text: The LORD: I am the LORD your God. Do not after the doings of Egypt nor Canaan, nor walk in their ordinances. Do My judgments, keep My ordinances. Keep My statutes; a man that does them shall live. I am the LORD. Guidance: Israel is bracketed by two cultures that will both fail. Egypt behind, Canaan ahead — neither knows God's design. You shall live by Mine, says the LORD. Holiness is the chosen way between two seductions.

None shall uncover the nakedness of any near kin — I am the LORD. Not mother, father's wife, sister, grandchildren, father's wife's daughter, father's or mother's sister, father's brother's wife, daughter-in-law, brother's wife, woman and her daughter, wife's sister while the wife lives. Guidance: A long list with one purpose — protect the family from itself. Many of these unions had political or property motives. God draws a tight circle: love, but not where bloodlines or marriage already bind. The family is to be safe ground.

Approach not a woman in her uncleanness. Lie not with thy neighbor's wife. Give not thy children to Molech, nor profane God's name. Lie not with mankind as with womankind — abomination. Lie not with any beast — confusion. Guidance: God names what should not be touched. Adultery breaks marriage, child sacrifice breaks parenthood, same-sex acts and bestiality break the body-pattern He designed. None of these are minor — He calls them abomination and confusion.

Defile not yourselves — the nations I cast out defiled themselves. The land is defiled; the land vomits out its inhabitants. Keep My statutes. Whoever commits these shall be cut off. Commit not abominable customs done before. I am the LORD your God. Guidance: Land itself can vomit out a people. The Canaanites did these things and lost the land; if Israel does the same, the land won't notice the difference. God's commands are not arbitrary — they keep a people fit to dwell.
